methodology.description: Assessment based on official government reports, international indices, academic research, NGO reports, and documented social media sentiment analysis. Scores range from 1 (very high discrimination risk) to 10 (very inclusive/low risk).
methodology.disclaimer: These assessments reflect documented patterns and published research as of early 2026. Individual experiences vary significantly. This is not legal advice. Always verify current conditions before relocating.

scoringScale.9-10: Very inclusive — strong legal protections, positive social attitudes, active integration programs
scoringScale.7-8: Generally inclusive — good legal framework, occasional incidents, supportive expat community
scoringScale.5-6: Mixed — legal protections exist but social attitudes vary, some documented incidents
scoringScale.3-4: Concerning — weak enforcement, notable social tensions, frequent documented incidents
scoringScale.1-2: High risk — systemic discrimination, poor legal protections, hostile social environment

languageAccessibility.note: English-speaking country; Portugal (score 8) best among non-English countries
